steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice best emphasizes when she collected both samples. Two key constraints: (1) timing must be present for each sample, and (2) both samples must appear — not just one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Thousands of botanical samples… online at the C. V. Starr Virtual Herbarium’: Focuses on where the samples can now be found. Gives no collection dates at all. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘While both specimens… Trixis antimenorrhoea was found in Minas Gerais and Stephanomeria thurberi was found in Chihuahua’: Emphasizes the locations of the two specimens, not the dates. No timing information. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘On June 26 in 1930… Trixis antimenorrhoea of the Asteraceae family’: Gives a date, but only for one sample (Trixis antimenorrhoea). The second specimen is absent. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Ynés Mexía collected a sample of Trixis antimenorrhoea in June of 1930, after collecting Stephanomeria thurberi in May of 1929’: Gives the collection date for both samples and uses “after” to show their timing relative to each other. Satisfies both constraints. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
